chunk_summary: Guidelines for feeding infants under 2 years during fever and vomiting, focusing on hypoglycemia prevention during illness, hospitalization, and recovery.
learning_objectives: Understand feeding strategies for infants under 2 years during fever and vomiting.
Learn methods to prevent hypoglycemia in sick infants and young children.
Identify key recommendations for feeding during illness and recovery phases.
Apply WHO and IMNCI guidelines for managing feeding in hospitalized children.
key_facts: Infants with fever or vomiting are at high risk of hypoglycemia and require frequent feeding.
Small, frequent meals or breastfeeding every 2-3 hours are recommended during illness.
Oral rehydration solutions (ORS) or clear fluids should be given to prevent dehydration.
WHO guidelines emphasize continued breastfeeding during illness to maintain nutrition.
Hypoglycemia can occur within 4-6 hours of fasting in young children, necessitating prompt feeding.
IMNCI guidelines recommend monitoring for signs of dehydration and hypoglycemia in sick children.
reference_quote: WHO guidelines recommend frequent feeding and continued breastfeeding during illness to prevent hypoglycemia and maintain nutrition (https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789241548373).
